headline: ROTATION CONFIRMATION (first agent assessment). Live composite 65.6 (Established). Note: scanner rotation_state listed 68.6; using live data per assessor protocol — DATA INTEGRITY FLAG. In-window evidence: March 2026 Council of Europe Advisory Committee assessment urging Portugal to advance Roma inclusion (employment, housing discrimination, women/youth-specific support, 'forced nomadism' concern, 2024 re-establishment of equality commission but Roma Advisory Group + Observatory ceased to function); Portugal late-2025/early-2026 Nationality + Immigration Law overhaul including 2-year family reunification rule; IOM 600-refugee resettlement commitment 2024-2026; ~13% population in overcrowded housing (27.7% among at-risk). EU-aligned democratic governance with rights-protection deficits in Roma inclusion + housing. Composite 65.6 (Established mid-band) appropriately reflects this. No 5pt-threshold-crossing acute event. Composite holds.
data_integrity_flag: Scanner rotation_state listed Portugal at 68.6; live data is 65.6. Used live per assessor protocol.

evidence.finding: Advisory Committee urges Portugal to advance Roma inclusion; specific concerns on employment discrimination, housing discrimination, forced nomadism, dissolution of Roma Advisory Group + Observatory, insufficient funding for Roma organizations.
October 2025 parliamentary approval + December 2025 Constitutional Court adjustments; 2-year family reunification rule with key exceptions; effective late 2025/early 2026.
AIMA-funded IOM partnership to resettle 600 refugees originally seeking asylum in Egypt and Turkey.

negativeSignals: March 2026 Council of Europe Roma inclusion deficit + dissolution of Roma support bodies + housing discrimination + 13%/27.7% overcrowding.
positiveSignals: Active refugee resettlement program; 'humanist but rigorous' migration policy framing; constitutional-court adjustment of immigration overhaul shows institutional checks.
